draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer interface depth created with subtle gradients and blur over hard contrast edges
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Explore softer, more layered visual hierarchy with gentle transitions between interface elements
facade.rejectImplies: Test sharper contrast, flatter planes, and more pronounced elevation differences in next probe

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to warm, approachable language than corporate formality in CRM interfaces
facade.label: Friendly, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace casual language, conversational microcopy, and warm personality throughout the product
facade.rejectImplies: Adopt formal, business-standard language and neutral, corporate tone for all user-facing text

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer interface depth created with subtle gradients and blur over hard contrast edges
evidence.implication: Explore softer, more layered visual hierarchy with gentle transitions between interface elements

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need high-contrast separation between navigation (persistent, dark) and workspace (bright, scannable) to reduce cognitive load when switching between clients and tasks
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light content
facade.acceptImplies: Future probes should explore persistent left-side navigation patterns, high-contrast UI separation, and dark-on-light workflows
facade.rejectImplies: Future probes should explore full-screen layouts, tab-based navigation, or minimal visual distinction between nav and content

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, zero-delay interactions over smooth transitions when managing time-sensitive client work
facade.label: Instant load, no animation
facade.acceptImplies: Build for speed-first UX: instant state changes, minimal motion, click-and-done workflows
facade.rejectImplies: Explore motion-rich feedback: transitions that confirm actions, animated state changes, gestural microinteractions

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prioritize glanceable metrics (revenue, active clients, pending invoices) over narrative context
facade.label: Dashboard with big numbers
facade.acceptImplies: User wants fast-scan status, likely prefers cards/tiles with key metrics, KPI-style layout
facade.rejectImplies: User wants context and detail upfront, likely prefers prose summaries, timeline views, or annotated reports

facade.hypothesis: For a CRM targeting freelancers, an organic, tactile aesthetic (natural materials, warm tones, handcrafted feel) might resonate more than sleek/sterile interfaces—suggesting freelancers value human connection and comfort over corporate polish.
facade.label: Warm wooden desk
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into natural textures, warm neutrals, and craft-like UI details (irregular borders, soft shadows, natural typography).
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cleaner, more minimalist or modern aesthetic—possibly cooler tones, geometric precision, digital-first visual language.

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information panels vs. card-based, modular layouts—reveals if they want to see everything at once or navigate through focused views
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step

axes.label: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool or a supportive coach
Whether they prefer calm, neutral atmosphere vs. motivational, energizing atmosphere—reveals if they want a quiet workspace or one that celebrates wins
Whether they want client data front-and-center vs. financial/project metrics prioritized—reveals what information matters most to their workflow
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered animations—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and delight

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information panels vs. card-based, modular layouts—reveals if they want to see everything at once or navigate through focused views
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool or a supportive coach
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, neutral atmosphere vs. motivational, energizing atmosphere—reveals if they want a quiet workspace or one that celebrates wins
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want client data front-and-center vs. financial/project metrics prioritized—reveals what information matters most to their workflow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered animations—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and delight
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers find single-color card designs with tonal variation more calming and less visually overwhelming than multi-colored interface elements, reducing cognitive load during client management tasks
facade.axisTargeted: Color complexity vs. simplicity in interface components
facade.label: Monochrome cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore muted, single-hue card systems with depth created through tone shifts rather than color variety
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers need color coding and visual differentiation across interface cards—pursue more vibrant or varied color palettes for quick scanning
